AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-31-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS RAJIV BEHTI, BERTORELLI, GANDI, WON & BEHTI AND RAJIV BEHIT, CPA INC'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Appearances:
Alton Burkhalter of Burkhalter Kessler Goodman & George (949.975.7500) for plaintiff and responding party
B. Douglas Robbins of O'Leary & O'Leary (247.7900) for defendants and moving parties

Argued and the Court ruled as follows: The motion is denied. Defendants have shown that plaintiffs had notice and concerns about a series of acts of Ms. Hillyer beginning in 2001 through 2005. Defendants have not shown that any of those events form the basis of plaintiffs' claim against defendants and/or constitute acts of embezzlement by Ms. Hillyer. As a consequence, it is the Court's conclusion that defendants failed to shift the burden of producing evidence on the statute of limitations issue. Further, the evidence reflects triable issues of fact as to what plaintiffs reasonably knew or should have know particularly given the fact that plaintiffs relied on defendants to address several of the issues of concern.
With respect to the negligent misrepresentation claim, the engagement letter does not state that it is a fully integrated agreement that defines the parties' relationship. As a consequence, there is a triable issue of fact as to defendants' representations and plaintiffs' reliance. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/AY) Judge: Patrick Mahoney; Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR #2542

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories; For Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-15-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
B. Douglas Robbins, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Rajiv Behti

NO OTHER APPEARANCE STATED. HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted with Modification: Defendant Rajiv Behti's motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories is granted in part. The motion is moot as to interrogatories #19-21 and 39-45. The general objections are overruled and stricken. The Code of Civil Procedure does not authorize general objections. The other objections are similarly overruled and stricken. Plaintiffs are ordered to serve supplemental verified responses within 30 days of notice of this order. The Court finds that plaintiffs asserted objections that lacked merit. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $1,560 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order.
MODIFICATION: That parties will meet and confer to narrow #26 and 31. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.
